items.final_wording: Supports contact-dermatitis terminology, itch, burning, erythema, scale, vesicles, blistering, edema, weeping, distribution, exposure, occupation, products, temporal course, atopy, and clinician-observed morphology.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Allergic Contact Dermatitis
source.population: People with suspected or clinician-diagnosed allergic contact dermatitis, including occupational, home, hobby, topical-product, and age-related exposure contexts.
source.setting: DHA-licensed telehealth assessment with face-to-face, family-physician, or dermatology referral where indicated.
source.exclusions: Direct for selected allergic contact-dermatitis history, morphology, exposure, investigation, red-flag, and documentation-only management fields. Irritant dermatitis, diagnosis, treatment, medicine, dose, referral urgency, and fixed follow-up remain clinician responsibilities.

exact_location.heading: Sections 1, 5, 6 and 9 — risks, morphology, distribution, symptoms, diagnosis, and history

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports contact-dermatitis terminology, itch, burning, erythema, scale, vesicles, blistering, edema, weeping, distribution, exposure, occupation, products, temporal course, atopy, and clinician-observed morphology.

items.rationale: The UAE DHA guideline supports documenting exposure timing, suspected contact product or occupation, distribution, morphology, symptoms, recurrence, impact, and change without identifying an allergen automatically.

items.final_wording: Supports fever or systemic illness, respiratory distress, rapidly worsening painful eczema, recurrent or spreading infection, extensive involvement, pregnancy, and clinician-led return or referral documentation.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Allergic Contact Dermatitis
source.population: People with suspected or clinician-diagnosed allergic contact dermatitis, including occupational, home, hobby, topical-product, and age-related exposure contexts.
source.setting: DHA-licensed telehealth assessment with face-to-face, family-physician, or dermatology referral where indicated.
source.exclusions: Direct for selected allergic contact-dermatitis history, morphology, exposure, investigation, red-flag, and documentation-only management fields. Irritant dermatitis, diagnosis, treatment, medicine, dose, referral urgency, and fixed follow-up remain clinician responsibilities.

exact_location.heading: Sections 7 and 13 — red flags and referral criteria

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports fever or systemic illness, respiratory distress, rapidly worsening painful eczema, recurrent or spreading infection, extensive involvement, pregnancy, and clinician-led return or referral documentation.

items.rationale: The red-flag section supports recording assessed mucosal, widespread, severe, infected, or systemic concern and clinician decisions without asserting those findings.

items.final_wording: Supports clinician-reviewed exposure history, photographs, patch testing, KOH or culture, and selected tests when diagnosis is uncertain, without recommending testing automatically.

exact_location.heading: Section 9 — patch testing, laboratory tests, photographs, and histopathology

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-reviewed exposure history, photographs, patch testing, KOH or culture, and selected tests when diagnosis is uncertain, without recommending testing automatically.

items.rationale: Investigation and patch-test context was reviewed but no test is generated.
